mnemonics.title: COQ Components
mnemonics.content: PAF - Prevention, Appraisal, Failure - Costs add up quickly, fix early!

mnemonics.title: Quality vs QC
mnemonics.content: QA = Qrevent Avoid defects (proactive), QC = Qheck Catch defects (reactive)

mnemonics.title: Six Sigma DMAIC
mnemonics.content: Don't Make Any Improvements Crude - Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control

mnemonics.title: CMMI Levels
mnemonics.content: Initial Repeat Define Quantify Optimize - Levels 1 to 5

acronyms.usage: Total cost of preventing, detecting, and fixing defects

visualTips.visualization: Think of three pillars: Prevention (foundation), Appraisal (monitoring), Failure (expensive roof collapse) - the higher the failure pillar, the more the roof collapses!

visualTips.visualize: A horizontal timeline from Planning → Design → Implementation → Testing → Maintenance, with quality gates at each stage like checkpoints on a race track
Steps of a staircase: Initial (chaos) → Managed (repeat) → Defined (standardize) → Quantitatively Managed (measure) → Optimizing (improve) - climb to the top!
An exponential curve rising sharply from requirements (low cost) to post-release (very high cost) - like a rocket taking off!

analogies.analogy: Quality in project planning is like foundation in building a house - you can add walls later, but a weak foundation will eventually collapse everything.
QA is like preventive medicine (healthy lifestyle, checkups), while QC is like diagnostic medicine (treating illness after symptoms appear).
Quality costs are like insurance premiums - paying for prevention (premiums) is much cheaper than paying for failure (claims).
CMMI levels are like learning to drive: Level 1 (novice, chaotic), Level 2 (can follow routes), Level 3 (knows the rules), Level 4 (drives efficiently), Level 5 (teaches others to drive better).
